title: TOI - Breaking News,  Latest News, India News, World News, Bollywood, Sports, Business and Political News | The Times of India
meta_description: Breaking News in India: Read Latest News on Sports, Business, Entertainment, Blogs and Opinions from leading columnists. Times of India brings the Breaking News and Latest News.

The focus will be on ensuring uninterrupted supply, stable logistics, and efficient distribution of petroleum, power, and fertilisers across the nation. This comes as the ongoing conflict impacts global energy supplies and international supply chains. US temporarily eases Iran oil sanctions. How and why this matters for India Indian refiners will hope they can purchase Iranian oil to ease pressure on domestic fuel prices, inflation and rupee. However, it may be easier said than done. While biology is understood, outcomes depend on care, early intervention, and societal perception. Early diagnosis and therapies are crucial for individuals to achieve milestones and participate fully in life. Virat Kohli reacts to viral 'chartered London flights' rumour ahead of IPL 2026 Virat Kohli has debunked rumors about requesting chartered flights for travel between India and the UK during IPL 2026. The RCB icon dismissed the speculation with laughing emojis on Instagram. The IPL 2026 season begins March 28, with RCB defending their title. ​Blue saree brigade: Women at the heart of India’s water systems Women make up less than 17 percent of the paid water workforce globally — but in India's villages, they are already running the systems. This is the story of how grassroots networks and government schemes are turning water's biggest burden-bearers into its most effective managers. How Denmark planned to counter a US attempt to invade Greenland Denmark’s public broadcaster first published information about plans that included blowing up airfields if the US attempted to carry out an invasion. The 3-word message Steve Jobs sent to his employees the day Apple beat Microsoft In 2010, Apple's market capitalization surpassed Microsoft's, a significant achievement after its near-bankruptcy in 1997. Steve Jobs, acknowledging the milestone, quoted Walt Disney to emphasize the company's focus on future innovation. While Apple and Alphabet now trail Nvidia, this 2010 event marked a pivotal shift in tech leadership. Why Mumbai court acquitted bizman in 2013 suicide case A Mumbai court acquitted a businessman accused of abetting his HIV-positive ex-wife's suicide, ruling mere harassment insufficient. The 12-year case hinged on allegations of abuse and threats. The judge emphasized the need for clear intent and direct instigation, finding prosecution evidence lacking on both counts, leading to reasonable doubt and acquittal. The Unspoken Tour Season 1: When poetry, stories, and truth take centre stage across India The Unspoken Tour – Season 1 is a premier national spoken-word IP bringing the emotional power of poetry to India’s grandest stages. Featuring Nayab Midha, Amandeep Khayal, and Priya Malik, the tour gives voice to the feelings and truths often left unsaid in today’s fast, digital world. Tesla finance VP Sendil Palani exits, recalls Tesla Deathwatch' days After seventeen years, Tesla's senior finance member Sendil Palani has departed, having joined when the company was on the brink of collapse in late 2008. Palani lauded his finance team for making complex tasks appear effortless and credited cross-functional late-night sessions for invaluable real-world learning. He thanked CEO Elon Musk for championing first-principles thinking. 09:03 After Trump’s 48-hr ultimatum, Iran says ships can pass Hormuz except ‘enemy’ vessels Jeff Bezos' ex-wife makes another 'record-setting gift Panchang Today, March 23, 2026: Shukla Paksh 5 (Panchami), Krittika, Shubh Muhurat, Rahu Kaal and more March 23, 2026, a Monday, aligns with the Moon and inner steadiness. The day features Shukla Paksh 5 (Panchami) and Krittika Nakshatra, favoring skill-building, organization, and direct communication. Taurus Moon encourages stability and planning, while Priti Yoga after midday brings ease and goodwill. Auspicious timings like Abhijit Muhurta support important tasks, but caution is advised during Rahu Kaal. Aishwarya Offer Condolences Next 1 2 The Voice of Conscience Previous The charkha as a weapon Margaret Bourke-White (Photographer) TOI take: Published on: Jan 22, 2026 While imprisoned at Yeravda Jail in Pune from 1932 to 1933, Mahatma Gandhi turned confinement into resistance, urging Indians to spin their own cloth as a rejection of British rule. When photographer Margaret Bourke-White arrived to capture his portrait, she was first required to learn how to use a charkha. The iconic portrait of Gandhi reading beside his wheel became an enduring symbol of his pacifist philosophy and non-violent civil disobedience. 'The last Jew in Vinnitsa' 1941 TOI take: Published on: Jan 21, 2026 Found in a German soldier’s album, this 1941 photograph titled "The Last Jew in Vinnitsa" captures an Einsatzgruppe D member executing a Ukrainian man. This chilling image documents the Holocaust in Ukraine, where nearly one million Jews were murdered by Nazi forces, including the German Army and auxiliary groups. A satellite’s unlikely ride 1981 TOI take: Published on: Nov 21, 2025 On June 19, 1981, Isro launched its first communication satellite, Apple, aboard Ariane-1 from Kourou in French Guiana. This satellite, iconically, was transported on a bullock cart—a reflection of Isro's humble beginnings—just six years after Aryabhatta, when both the agency and the nation’s infrastructure were still developing.
